2605.21807.title: When Cases Get Rare: A Retrieval Benchmark for Off-Guideline Clinical Question Answering
2605.21807.paper_summary: Across medical specialties, clinical practice is anchored in evidence-based guidelines that codify best studied diagnostic and treatment pathways. These pathways routinely fall short for the long tail of real-world care not covered by guidelines. Most medical large language models (LLMs), however, are trained to encode common, guideline-focused medical knowledge in their parameters. Current evaluations test models primarily on recalling and reasoning with this memorized content, often in multiple-choice settings. Given the fundamental importance of evidence-based reasoning in medicine, it is neither feasible nor reliable to depend on memorization in practice. To address this gap, we introduce OGCaReBench, a free-form retrieval-focused benchmark aimed at evaluating LLMs at answering clinical questions that require going beyond typical guidelines. Extracted from published medical case reports and validated by medical experts, OGCaReBench contains long-form clinical questions requiring free-text answers, providing a systematic framework for assessing open-ended medical reasoning in rare, case-based scenarios. Our experiments reveal that even the best-performing baseline (GPT-5.2) correctly answers only 56% of our benchmark with specialized models only reaching 42%. Augmenting models with retrieved medical articles improves this performance to up to 82% (using GPT-5.2) highlighting the importance of evidence-grounding for real-world medical reasoning tasks. This work thus establishes a foundation for benchmarking and advancing both general-purpose and medical LLMs to produce reliable answers in challenging clinical contexts.
2605.21807.paper_summary_zh: 在各醫療專科中，臨床實踐是以基於證據的指導方針為基礎，這些指導方針編纂了最佳研究的診斷和治療途徑。這些途徑通常對於未被指導方針涵蓋的現實世界護理的長尾部分感到不足。然而，大多數醫療大型語言模型（LLMs）是被訓練來在其參數中編碼常見的、以指導方針為重點的醫療知識。目前的評估主要測試模型在回憶和推理這些記憶內容方面的能力，通常是在多選題的情境中進行。考慮到基於證據的推理在醫學中的根本重要性，依賴記憶在實踐中既不可行也不可靠。為了解決這一差距，我們引入了OGCaReBench，一個以自由形式檢索為重點的基準，旨在評估LLMs回答需要超越典型指導方針的臨床問題的能力。OGCaReBench提取自已發表的醫療案例報告，並經醫療專家驗證，包含需要自由文本答案的長篇臨床問題，為評估在稀有案例基礎情境中的開放式醫療推理提供了一個系統框架。我們的實驗顯示，即使是表現最佳的基準（GPT-5.2）也僅正確回答了我們基準的56%，而專門模型的正確率僅達到42%。通過檢索醫療文章來增強模型，這一表現提高到了82%（使用GPT-5.2），突顯了基於證據的現實世界醫療推理任務的重要性。因此，本研究為基準測試和推進通用及醫療LLMs在挑戰性臨床情境中產生可靠答案奠定了基礎。
2605.21807.author: Doeun Lee et.al.
2605.21807.authors: Doeun Lee, Muge Zhang, Yi Yu, Ashish Manne, Stephen Koesters, Frank Wen, Brady Buchanan, Lynda Villagomez, Oluwatoba Moninuola, James Lim, Kathryn Tobin, Andrew Srisuwananukorn, Ping Zhang, Sachin Kumar
